syntaxReference: Program skeleton: public class MySketch extends GraphicsProgram { public void run() { /* ... */ } }
Add objects: GRect r = new GRect(80,40); add(r, 100, 120);
Colors and fill: rect.setFilled(true); rect.setFillColor(Color.BLUE); rect.setColor(Color.BLACK);
Canvas size: double w = getWidth(); double h = getHeight();
Text: GLabel lbl = new GLabel("Hello"); lbl.setFont("SansSerif-18"); add(lbl, x, y);
Images: GImage img = new GImage("pic.png"); add(img, x, y); img.scale(0.5);
Groups: GCompound c = new GCompound(); c.add(new GOval(20,20), 0, 0); add(c, 50, 50);
Mouse input: addMouseListeners(); public void mousePressed(MouseEvent e) { /* ... */ }
Keyboard input: addKeyListeners(); public void keyPressed(KeyEvent e) { if (e.getKeyCode()==KeyEvent.VK_LEFT) {...} }
Collision sample: GObject hit = getElementAt(x, y); if (hit != null) { /* collide */ }
Fonts and metrics: lbl.getAscent() to baseline-align labels (y is baseline).
commonPatterns: Game loop with velocity: double dx=3, dy=2; while(true){ obj.move(dx,dy); if(hitWall()) dx=-dx; pause(16); }
Drag-and-drop: on mousePressed->selected=getElementAt; store offset; on mouseDragged->selected.setLocation(e.getX()-offX, e.getY()-offY);
Keyboard movement: addKeyListeners(); keyPressed-> if(VK_LEFT) player.move(-SPEED,0); if(VK_RIGHT) player.move(SPEED,0);
Centering object: add(obj, getWidth()/2 - obj.getWidth()/2, getHeight()/2 - obj.getHeight()/2);
Point-sampling collision: GObject hit = getElementAt(ball.getX()+r, ball.getY()+r); if(hit!=null){ dy=-dy; }
Boundary clamp: x = Math.max(0, Math.min(x, getWidth()-obj.getWidth())); obj.setLocation(x, obj.getY());
Parallax/background: background.sendToBack(); move foreground faster than background for depth effect.
Timer alternative: new javax.swing.Timer(16, e->{ step(); }).start(); // if using Swing timer instead of while+pause
quickTips: Use small, consistent time steps (e.g., 16–20 ms) and tune speeds in pixels/frame for smooth motion.
Sample multiple points around fast-moving objects to avoid tunneling through thin paddles or walls.
Keep drawing and game logic separate; write helper methods like updateBall(), checkCollisions(), and handleInput() for clarity.
